communicative_purpose: To indicate the gate where passengers board their flight
trigger_situations: Airport navigation, flight announcements, boarding procedures
variations: la porte, le portail, la porte d'embarquement, porte B12
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'port' (harbor); in airports always refers to gate
collocates: numéro, B12, C3, retard,'embarquement en cours
example_1: Votre porte d'embarquement est la B12, au bout du terminal 2.
example_2: L'embarquement se fait à la porte C3 dans 20 minutes.
example_3: Je cherche la porte d'embarquement pour le vol Paris-Lyon.
example_1_translation: Your boarding gate is B12, at the end of Terminal 2.
example_2_translation: Boarding is at gate C3 in 20 minutes.
example_3_translation: I'm looking for the boarding gate for the Paris-Lyon flight.

communicative_purpose: To refer to luggage that goes in the aircraft hold
contexts: Airports, airlines, check-in counters, luggage handling
variations: les bagages en soute, les valises en soute, enregistrer les bagages
common_mistakes: 'Soute' means cargo hold, not trunk of car; confusing with 'bagages à main' (carry-on)
example_1: Vous avez droit à 23 kilos de bagages en soute sur ce vol.
example_2: Mes bagages en soute ont été enregistrés au comptoir 7.
example_3: Je préfère ne pas mettre tout en soute, j'ai des affaires fragiles.
example_1_translation: You are allowed 23 kilos of checked baggage on this flight.
example_2_translation: My checked baggage was checked in at counter 7.
example_3_translation: I prefer not to put everything in the hold, I have fragile things.

communicative_purpose: To announce that a flight departure is delayed
variations: le vol a du retard, le vol est annulé, départreporté
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'retardé' (held back, delayed); proper term is 'en retard' or 'retardé'
collocates: de 2 heures, cause météo, raisons techniques
example_1: Le vol AF123 est retardé de deux heures cause problèmes techniques.
example_2: Mon vol est retardé, je vais manquer ma correspondance.
example_3: Tous les vols sont retardés à cause de la tempête de neige.
example_1_translation: Flight AF123 is delayed by two hours due to technical problems.
example_2_translation: My flight is delayed, I'm going to miss my connection.
example_3_translation: All flights are delayed due to the snowstorm.

communicative_purpose: To indicate the action of checking in for a flight
trigger_situations: Airport check-in, online check-in, bag drop
contexts: Airports, airlines, departure halls, online services
variations: je m'enregistre, je fais le check-in, s'enregistrer en ligne
common_mistakes: 'Enregistrer' can also mean to register; context determines meaning
collocates: mon vol, en ligne, à l'aéroport, maintenant
example_1: Je fais enregistrer mon vol pour Madrid, départ à 14h.
example_2: On peut s'enregistrer en ligne 24 heures avant le vol.
example_3: Je me suis enregistré en ligne pour éviter la queue.
example_1_translation: I check in my flight for Madrid, departure at 2 p.m.
example_2_translation: You can check in online 24 hours before the flight.
example_3_translation: I checked in online to avoid the line.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the next flight in a journey after arriving from another
variations: la correspondance, le transit, l'escale, la connexion
common_mistakes: 'Correspondance' specifically means change of aircraft; 'escale' means stopover
collocates: dans 45 minutes, porter, attendre, prochain vol
example_1: J'ai une correspondance de 45 minutes à CDG, c'est juste.
example_2: Ma correspondance est à Terminal 2E, il faut prendre le shuttle.
example_3: Si le premier vol est retardé, je vais manquer ma correspondance.
example_1_translation: I have a 45 minute connection at CDG, that's fair.
example_2_translation: My connection is at Terminal 2E, you have to take the shuttle.
example_3_translation: If the first flight is delayed, I will miss my connection.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the security checkpoint at airports
trigger_situations: Airport security, going through checkpoints, screening
variations: le contrôle de sécurité, le security check, le scan, les contrôles
common_mistakes: English 'security check' increasingly used; 'contrôle' is correct French
example_1: Le contrôle de sécurité est au bout du couloir, à gauche.
example_2: Préparez vos documents, le contrôle de sécurité prend du temps.
example_3: On doit vider nos liquides avant le contrôle de sécurité.
example_1_translation: The security checkpoint is at the end of the corridor, on the left.
example_2_translation: Prepare your documents, the security check takes time.
example_3_translation: We have to empty our liquids before the security check.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the document needed to board an aircraft
trigger_situations: Boarding, check-in, gate access, airport navigation
contexts: Airports, airlines, boarding gates, travel documents
variations: la boarding pass, le pass, la carte d'embarquement, le billet
common_mistakes: English 'boarding pass' commonly used in French; 'carte d'embarquement' is official
example_1: J'ai ma carte d'embarquement sur mon téléphone, c'est plus pratique.
example_2: Vous devez présenter votre carte d'embarquement à la porte.
example_3: Je n'ai pas pu imprimer ma carte d'embarquement à la maison.
example_1_translation: I have my boarding pass on my phone, it's more practical.
example_2_translation: You must present your boarding pass at the gate.
example_3_translation: I was unable to print my boarding pass at home.

communicative_purpose: To indicate a flight will depart at the scheduled time
trigger_situations: Flight status checks, travel updates, scheduling
contexts: Airports, airlines, departure boards, travel updates
variations: le vol part à l'heure, pas de retard, départ prévu
common_mistakes: Confusing departure and arrival times; context important
example_1: Le vol Paris-Rome part à l'heure, pas de retard prévu.
example_2: D'après le tableau, le départ est à l'heure, c'est une bonne nouvelle.
example_3: Tous les vols sont à l'heure ce matin, pas de perturbations.
example_1_translation: The Paris-Rome flight leaves on time, no delays expected.
example_2_translation: According to the table, the departure is on time, which is good news.
example_3_translation: All flights are on time this morning, no disruptions.

communicative_purpose: To refer to an airline company
trigger_situations: Booking flights, airline references, travel discussions
contexts: Airlines, airports, travel agencies, flight bookings
variations: la airline, la Cie aérienne, la société aérienne, Air France
common_mistakes: English 'airline' used in French; 'compagnie aérienne' is correct
collocates: Air France, EasyJet, Ryanair, nationale, internationale
example_1: Quelle compagnie aérienne prenez-vous pour aller à Berlin ?
example_2: La compagnie aérienne propose un service snack à bord.
example_3: Je préfère voyager avec une compagnie aérienne reconnue.
example_1_translation: Which airline do you take to Berlin?
example_2_translation: The airline offers an on-board snack service.
example_3_translation: I prefer to travel with a recognized airline.

communicative_purpose: To refer to an exclusive airport lounge for premium passengers
trigger_situations: Airport lounges, business class, premium services
contexts: Airports, business travel, lounges, exclusive areas
variations: le salon business, le lounge, la salle VIP, le salon première classe
common_mistakes: 'Salon' and 'lounge' both used; context determines exclusivity level
collocates: accès, business class, frequent flyer, gratuit
example_1: Les passagers en business class ont accès au salon VIP.
example_2: On peut payer pour accéder au salon VIP, environ 50 euros.
example_3: Le salon VIP offre des snacks, boissons et WiFi gratuit.
example_1_translation: Business class passengers have access to the VIP lounge.
example_2_translation: You can pay to access the VIP lounge, around 50 euros.
example_3_translation: The VIP lounge offers snacks, drinks and free WiFi.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the moving belt where luggage arrives
trigger_situations: Baggage claim, luggage collection, arrival halls
contexts: Airports, baggage reclaim areas, luggage handling
variations: le carousel, le tapis de livraison, la bande transporteuse
common_mistakes: 'Carousel' is commonly used French term; 'tapis roulant' is more general
example_1: Les bagages arrivent sur le tapis roulant numéro 4.
example_2: J'attends mes bagages sur le tapis roulant depuis 20 minutes.
example_3: Le tapis roulant est en panne, ils vont livrer les bagages manuellement.
example_1_translation: The luggage arrives on conveyor belt number 4.
example_2_translation: I've been waiting for my luggage on the conveyor belt for 20 minutes.
example_3_translation: The conveyor belt is broken, they will deliver the luggage manually.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the international departure/arrival area
trigger_situations: Transit between countries, border controls, customs
variations: la zone internationale, le secteur international, zone transit
common_mistakes: Confusing with domestic zone; international requires passport and customs
example_1: La zone internationale est au terminal 2E, après les contrôles.
example_2: Il faut passer les contrôles pour accéder à la zone internationale.
example_3: En zone internationale, on peut faire des achats hors taxes.
example_1_translation: The international zone is at terminal 2E, after controls.
example_2_translation: You have to go through controls to access the international zone.
example_3_translation: In international areas, you can make tax-free purchases.

communicative_purpose: To indicate there are no more seats available on a flight
trigger_situations: Booking flights, waiting lists, travel planning
variations: plus de places, complet, completement booké, surbooké
common_mistakes: Completing with other flight; 'surbooké' means overbooked (negative)
collocates: aucune place, liste d'attente, autre vol
example_1: Le vol de ce soir est complet, vous êtes sur liste d'attente.
example_2: Tous les vols pour demain sont complets,Try Wednesday.
example_3: Il y a encore une place en classe affaires si ça vous intéresse.
example_1_translation: Tonight's flight is full, you are on the waiting list.
example_2_translation: All flights for tomorrow are full, Try Wednesday.
example_3_translation: There is still a seat in business class if you are interested.

meaning: to queue / to wait in line
communicative_purpose: To indicate waiting in a line or queue
trigger_situations: Airport lines, check-in queues, passport control
variations: attendre dans la queue, faire la file, être dans la file
common_mistakes: 'Queue' means tail in English; in French it means line (like the animal)
example_1: Il faut faire la queue pour le contrôle de sécurité, au moins 45 minutes.
example_2: La queue au check-in est très longue ce matin.
example_3: On a fait la queue pendant une heure pour avoir nos passeport vérifiés.
example_1_translation: You have to queue for the security check, at least 45 minutes.
example_2_translation: The check-in line is very long this morning.
example_3_translation: We waited in line for an hour to have our passports checked.

communicative_purpose: To refer to shops selling tax-free goods, usually after security
trigger_situations: Airport shopping, tax-free purchases, luxury goods
variations: la boutique hors taxes, le duty-free shop, les achats duty-free
common_mistakes: English 'duty-free' very common in French; official term is 'boutique hors taxes'
example_1: Je vais faire un tour au duty free pour acheter du parfum.
example_2: Les boutiques duty free sont ouvertes 24h à CDG.
example_3: On peut faire des économies au duty free si on voyage hors Europe.
example_1_translation: I'm going to go to duty free to buy some perfume.
example_2_translation: Duty free shops are open 24 hours in CDG.
example_3_translation: You can save money at duty free if you travel outside Europe.
